description: Interactive zoom controls for 2D graphs. zoomin creates a button that zooms into a target point or object. zoomout creates a button that resets the zoom to the original view. Both require at() as the first argument for button placement.
utility: Create interactive buttons for zooming in to a target or resetting zoom on a g2d graph

arguments.name: position
arguments.description: Logical position for the zoom button using at(row, col)

arguments.name: target
arguments.description: For zoomin: coordinates, point, or object to zoom into

arguments.name: scale
arguments.description: Zoom scale for zoomin (default 0.5 = zoom to 50% of range)

alternates.description: Button that zooms into a specific point with default scale

alternates.syntax: zoomin(at(row, col), graph, x, y, scale)
alternates.description: Button that zooms into a point with custom scale factor
Button that zooms into a previously defined point

alternates.variant: Zoom in to point with scale
alternates.description: Button that zooms to a point with custom scale
Button that zooms into the center of a circle, polygon, or other object
Button that resets zoom to original view with default 1s duration
Button that resets zoom with custom animation duration

examples.scenario: Zoom in button for a polygon region
examples.expression:
G = g2d(at(2,2), 20, 30, range(-1, 10), type(plotting))
P = polygon(G, point(G, 0, 0), point(G, 4, 0), point(G, 0, 3))
zoomin(at(25,2), G, P)

examples.scenario: Zoom in button to specific coordinates
Zoom in button with custom scale
Zoom out button to reset view
Zoom out button with fast animation
